name: Vonage API (Nexmo)
what_it_does: Communications API platform (formerly Nexmo) offering SMS, voice, video, and messaging APIs for building communication features into applications.
use_cases: Sending SMS notifications from agent workflows
Voice call automation and IVR systems
Two-factor authentication via SMS or voice
not_for: Teams already committed to Twilio (switching cost is high)
best_when: An agent needs multi-channel communications (SMS + voice + video) from a single provider with competitive global pricing.
avoid_when: You only need email, or you need a simpler pricing model than Vonage's complex rate cards.

auth.notes: SMS API uses API key + secret. Voice and other APIs use JWT. Two different auth methods depending on which API you use - adds complexity for agents.

pricing.notes: Trial credit available without credit card. After exhaustion, requires top-up. Generally competitive pricing vs Twilio for international.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error messages are functional but less polished than Twilio. Some legacy Nexmo terminology persists in error codes.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency support. Track message-uuid from responses.
known_agent_gotchas: Two different auth systems (API key vs JWT) depending on which API - confusing for agents
Legacy Nexmo and modern Vonage APIs coexist with different base URLs
Dashboard is less intuitive than Twilio for debugging
International number availability varies significantly by country
